title: Huperzia × koolauensis
taxonomy: Huperzia × koolauensis is a hybrid species of the genus Huperzia, a group of liverwort plants. It is a rare species found in Hawaii. The classification of Huperzia × koolauensis is a topic of ongoing research. It is believed to be a hybrid of two species within the genus Huperzia, but the exact parent species have not been identified. Further study is needed to confirm the classification of this unique plant species.
uses: Huperzia × koolauensis is a rare and unique plant species.  However, Huperzia × koolauensis is known to contain huperzine A, a compound with potential neuroprotective properties. intro: Huperzia × koolauensis is a rare, sterile hybrid plant species within the Huperzia genus, commonly known as a liverwort. This plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, and has been shown to have potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties. The bioactive constituents of Huperzia × koolauensis include huperzine A and B, as well as other alkaloids that contribute to its medicinal properties. Huperzia × koolauensis is often used in herbal preparations, including capsules, teas, and extracts, which are sold as dietary supplements to support cognitive function, memory, and overall well-being.
